introSection: TMNT Shellraiser is a steel launched roller coaster currently at Nickelodeon Universe American Dream in East Rutherford, New Jersey, USA. It is a Gerstlauer Euro-Fighter 1000, which opened on October 25, 2019. It is based on Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles. The shellraiser is a vehicle used by the characters. The ride has a lighting package from KCL Engineering.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Photographs of dark green track at a manufacturing facility were posted online in April 2016. It was speculated online that they belonged to a clone of Takabisha.

Gerstlauer and Triple Five Group officially revealed this coaster and an intertwined spinning coaster in November 2017. A model of the two roller coasters was on display during the 2017 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

Vertical construction of the attraction officially began in early 2018. The name was revealed during the American Coaster Enthusiast's Eastcoaster event on February 2, 2019.

The roller coaster opened with the park on October 25, 2019.

The ride was standing but not operating from January 9, 2024 through September 17, 2025.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
TMNT Shellraiser is a near clone of Takabisha at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan, the only difference being that the drop is half a degree steeper. It begins with a pre-launch section that includes a heartline roll before going through an LSM launch that launches from 0- in 2 seconds. The cars enter a tall corkscrew, then a banana roll, then another corkscrew. The second half of the ride begins with a vertical chain lift hill and a beyond vertical drop at an angle of 121.5 degrees, the steepest drop in the world. This is immediately followed by a dive loop, inside top hat, and an Immelmann.

The roller coaster is intertwined with Shredder, a Gerstlauer spinning roller coaster.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Riders are held in by over-the-shoulder harnesses.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Ride page on the park's website

fullText:
TMNT Shellraiser is a steel launched roller coaster currently at Nickelodeon Universe American Dream in East Rutherford, New Jersey, USA. It is a Gerstlauer Euro-Fighter 1000, which opened on October 25, 2019. It is based on Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles. The shellraiser is a vehicle used by the characters. The ride has a lighting package from KCL Engineering.

Photographs of dark green track at a manufacturing facility were posted online in April 2016. It was speculated online that they belonged to a clone of Takabisha.

Gerstlauer and Triple Five Group officially revealed this coaster and an intertwined spinning coaster in November 2017. A model of the two roller coasters was on display during the 2017 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

Vertical construction of the attraction officially began in early 2018. The name was revealed during the American Coaster Enthusiast's Eastcoaster event on February 2, 2019.

The roller coaster opened with the park on October 25, 2019.

The ride was standing but not operating from January 9, 2024 through September 17, 2025.

TMNT Shellraiser is a near clone of Takabisha at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan, the only difference being that the drop is half a degree steeper. It begins with a pre-launch section that includes a heartline roll before going through an LSM launch that launches from 0- in 2 seconds. The cars enter a tall corkscrew, then a banana roll, then another corkscrew. The second half of the ride begins with a vertical chain lift hill and a beyond vertical drop at an angle of 121.5 degrees, the steepest drop in the world. This is immediately followed by a dive loop, inside top hat, and an Immelmann.

The roller coaster is intertwined with Shredder, a Gerstlauer spinning roller coaster.

Riders are held in by over-the-shoulder harnesses.




 * Ride page on the park's website
